2604.27859.title: Rethinking Agentic Reinforcement Learning In Large Language Models
2604.27859.paper_summary: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has traditionally focused on training specialized agents to optimize predefined reward functions within narrowly defined environments. However, the advent of powerful Large Language Models (LLMs) and increasingly complex, open-ended tasks has catalyzed a paradigm shift towards agentic paradigms within RL. This emerging framework extends beyond traditional RL by emphasizing the development of autonomous agents capable of goal-setting, long-term planning, dynamic strategy adaptation, and interactive reasoning in uncertain, real-world environments. Unlike conventional approaches that rely heavily on static objectives and episodic interactions, LLM-based Agentic RL incorporates cognitive-like capabilities such as meta-reasoning, self-reflection, and multi-step decision-making directly into the learning loop. In this paper, we provide a deep insight for looking the conceptual foundations, methodological innovations, and effective designs underlying this trend. Furthermore, we identify critical challenges and outline promising future directions for building LLM-based Agentic RL.
2604.27859.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習（RL）傳統上專注於訓練專門的代理人，以優化在狹窄定義的環境中預先定義的獎勵函數。<lb/>然而，強大的大型語言模型（LLMs）的出現以及日益複雜的開放式任務促使了RL中代理性範式的範式轉變。<lb/>這一新興框架超越了傳統的RL，強調開發能夠設定目標、進行長期規劃、動態調整策略以及在不確定的現實環境中進行互動推理的自主代理人。<lb/>與依賴靜態目標和情節互動的傳統方法不同，基於LLM的代理性RL將類似認知的能力，如元推理、自我反思和多步決策，直接納入學習循環中。<lb/>在本文中，我們深入探討了這一趨勢背後的概念基礎、方法創新和有效設計。<lb/>此外，我們還確定了關鍵挑戰，並概述了建立基於LLM的代理性RL的有前景的未來方向。
2604.27859.author: Fangming Cui et.al.
2604.27859.authors: Fangming Cui, Ruixiao Zhu, Cheng Fang, Sunan Li, Jiahong Li
